content:
4 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

As frontend developers, we’ve all written code like this:
window.addEventListener("resize", () => {
console.log("Window resized");
});It works perfectly when the browser window changes size.
But what if the browser window never changes, yet your component becomes larger or smaller?
This is where many developers first encounter the ResizeObserver API.
In this article, we’ll understand why ResizeObserver exists, when to use it, and build a practical example that demonstrates its real value.
The Problem
Imagine you’re building a dashboard.
+------------------------------------------------------+
| Header |
+------------+-----------------------------------------+
| Sidebar | |
| | Photo Gallery |
| | |
| | 🖼️ 🖼️ 🖼️ |
| | 🖼️ 🖼️ 🖼️ |
| | |
+------------+-----------------------------------------+
